target.name: 20 Microns Ltd.
target.activities: 20 Microns Limited is the pioneers in the field of Industrial Minerals in India and bringing the organized concept of Micronization and tapping various different Industrial Micronized and Sub-Micronized Minerals under its portfolio. Since few years back, the company also delved into the world of Specialty Minerals, Chemicals and Performance Additives catering to the niche segments and formulations made through advanced and superior technology to serve its existing and new customer base for diverse applications. The company has a strong presence across the country with dedicated regional divisions for commercial distribution and technical assistance along with a vast network and infrastructure. With a devoted R&D and Product Application Centre, the company is committed to continuously innovate and offer a variety of products catering to numerous applications and formulations enhancing the product performances and delivering high value added functional solutions to various industries. 20 Microns, through its innovative & continuous R&D thrives to add further more products into its basket of products with continuous interaction with its domestic and international customers which has always proven to be its key to success.

source_caveats: Borrowings, cash and current liabilities are not in the source extract: net debt is treated as 0 with a warning, and capital employed is approximated by segment capital-employed where reported, else Net Worth.
Market prices are not in the source extract: no trading multiples — the valuation runs on the disclosed book basis until a market-price feed is added.

valuation.discount_reason: no DLOM — target is listed/public (liquid equity)

peers_used.name: Universal MEP Projects & Engineering Services Ltd.

valuation.ev_basis: Primary basis: market enterprise value (market cap + net debt) of 0 LISTED comparables. Book capital-employed proxy (15 unlisted comps) is used only as a per-method fallback when fewer than 3 listed comps exist for a metric. Peer multiples are similarity-weighted (borderline comps count less). Multiples are sector-calibrated — peer book distributions re-levelled to published trading anchors (sector 'mining' trading anchors: ev/ebitda 9.0x · ev/sales 1.9x · ev/ebit 11.2x, size factor ×1.0 (jul-2026 vintage, calibrated to observed nse sector points)); replace the anchor table with a live market feed for exact levels.
valuation.positioning: target EBITDA margin 12.2% ranks at the 40th percentile of the peer set → central multiple taken at peer P40 (not the flat median).

comparability_adjustment.reason: target size within the peer scale band — no adjustment

notes: multiples are SECTOR-CALIBRATED — peer book distributions re-levelled to published trading anchors (sector 'MINING' trading anchors: EV/EBITDA 9.0x · EV/Sales 1.9x · EV/EBIT 11.2x, size factor ×1.0 (Jul-2026 vintage, calibrated to observed NSE sector points)); replace the anchor table with a live market feed for exact levels

data-source caveat: Market prices are not in the source extract: no trading multiples — the valuation runs on the disclosed book basis until a market-price feed is added.
warnings: equity value withheld — borrowings & cash not in the data source and nothing is assumed. The enterprise-value range is reported; supply the missing figures (chat / enrichment) to bridge EV → equity.

validation.basis: engine EV mid vs the company's own observed NSE market cap; EV~equity proxy disclosed (no borrowings/cash in source)
validation.note: Two known idiosyncratic outliers are retained deliberately; the ranges disagree with them honestly rather than being widened to swallow them.
